campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review the city's financial health in July 2025, looking at how the city's investments performed and where money was spent that month. They also discussed plans to improve transportation options around Campbell, including ways to make it easier for residents to get around by different methods like biking, walking, and public transit.
keyTopics: July 2025 investment performance and financial transactions
City treasurer's monthly financial report
New multimodal transportation plan (biking, walking, transit improvements)

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026, where residents could share their input on a proposed new rule or policy. After hearing from the public, the council moved forward by introducing an ordinance (a new city law) and skipped the usual first-reading step to speed up the process. The ordinance will come back for final approval at the next council meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather community input
New ordinance introduced and fast-tracked for adoption
Final vote scheduled for next regular council meeting
Specific ordinance topic not detailed in agenda summary

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026, where they reviewed and approved meeting minutes from previous sessions in March. The council also authorized the Town Manager to move forward with a construction contract with QLM, Inc., though the specific project details are not fully outlined in the available agenda information.
keyTopics: Approval of March meeting minutes from multiple council sessions
Construction contract authorization with QLM, Inc.
Town Manager given authority to execute the construction agreement

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 15, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to plan and organize upcoming council sessions. They reviewed what items would be added to the April 21st and April 28th meeting agendas, decided which items needed specific time slots, and checked on any special notice requirements for documents being discussed.
keyTopics: Planning the April 21, 2026 council meeting agenda
Planning the April 28, 2026 council meeting agenda
Assigning specific times for agenda items
Reviewing public records from early April 2026
VEBA Advisory Committee (benefits/insurance committee)

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The City Council held a planning session to review Mountain View's spending plans for the next fiscal year, focusing on large projects and infrastructure improvements. They also officially recognized Teen Month and National Volunteer Week to celebrate young people and community volunteers in the city.
keyTopics: Next year's budget for city construction and infrastructure projects
Teen Month recognition and celebration
National Volunteer Week proclamation
Capital improvement planning for 2026-27

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 14, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met to review past meeting notes and discuss changes to tenant protection rules in Sunnyvale. The main focus was introducing a new ordinance that would update how the city handles residential tenant protections.
keyTopics: Approval of previous meeting minutes from March 25, 2026
New ordinance to amend tenant protection policies in Chapter 19.71
Changes to the Residential Tenant Protections Program

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 16, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a meeting on April 16, 2026 where members of the public could speak about any city matters for up to 3 minutes each. The council also held elections to choose a new Chair and Vice Chair, and started discussions about the city's budget for the upcoming fiscal year.
keyTopics: Public comment period - residents could address the council on any city issue
Chair and Vice Chair elections for the council
Fiscal year budget presentation and input from council members
State law limits council decisions on matters not formally listed on the agenda

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 16, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council meeting on April 16, 2026 included important legal information for residents. The city disclosed that if you disagree with certain city decisions, you have 90 days from the decision date to file a lawsuit, otherwise you lose your right to challenge it in court.
keyTopics: 90-day deadline to sue the city over decisions
Legal challenge rules for city actions
Resident rights to contest city decisions
California Civil Procedure requirements

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to update the city's 5-year housing and community development plan, review requests from individual council members for new projects, and consider changing rules around how council vacancies are filled. The council also approved meeting minutes and previewed upcoming agenda items.
keyTopics: Updates to the 2025-2030 housing and community development plan
New project requests submitted by individual council members
Proposed changes to council vacancy fill procedures
Approval of previous meeting minutes and scheduling

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 9, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council held a public hearing about a building at 405 Kipling Street in downtown. The owner is asking the city to officially recognize the building as more historically important than it currently is, which would allow them to add 5,000 square feet of new space to the building as a reward for preserving and upgrading it to meet earthquake safety standards.
keyTopics: 405 Kipling Street historic designation upgrade request
5,000 sq ft bonus floor area for seismic and historic rehabilitation
Downtown commercial zone development incentive
Historic Resources Board meeting schedule approval
